title: (BUG) cli: in-flight file_transfers never expire or get cleared on reconnect
description: The file_transfers HashMap (transfer_id → FileReceiveState) is populated when a FileHeader arrives and entries are removed only when the last FileChunk arrives and assembly succeeds. Two problems: (1) It is never cleared on Reconnecting, so if a sender disconnects mid-transfer, the partial FileReceiveState (and its pre-allocated chunks Vec) lives forever in memory. Each entry pre-allocates vec![None; total_chunks as usize] up to 230 Nones (FILE_MAX_CHUNKS). (2) A malicious peer can open up to 2^122 concurrent transfers (Uuid key space) because there is no cap on the number of simultaneous in-flight transfers — only the per-transfer size is bounded. Fix: (a) clear file_transfers in the Reconnecting handler; (b) add a maximum concurrent-transfers cap (e.g., 16) and reject new FileHeaders when exceeded. Lines 1230 and 1460 in cli/src/commands.rs.
